gauge.name: North Fork Salt Creek (IN) at Nashville

impacts.statement: Major flooding in progress along North Fork Salt Creek in the Nashville area.  Flood levels similar to those seen on  June 7, 2008 and are a foot or more higher than those of July 2015.
Extensive flooding in progrees along the North Fork Salt Creek in the Nashville area.  Flood levels similar to those of July 13-14, 2015.
Extensive flooding along North Fork Salt Creek in the Nashville area. Flooding affects Brown County Jail and local busineses. Roads are closed.

Flooding becomes more extensive along North Fork Salt Creek in the Nashville area. Some roads are flooded and possibly a few businesses. This level is similar to the July 12, 2015 flood...the first of two floods to strike the Nashville area in just a few days. The following flood, less than two days later, was higher and more severe.
Significant flooding begins in the Nashville area.  High water closes at least one road.
Flooding in progress on at least one road in the Nashville area.
Flooding begins along much of the North Fork Salt Creek in the Nashville area.
High water starts over the gravel road to the Nashville Wastewater Treatment Plant at this level or slightly higher.
Approaching the top of the bank.  High water begins to affect low spots in agricultural fields.

dataAttribution.title: Observations courtesy of U.S. Geological Survey

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are not available. Only observed stages are available for this point.
